title: Tickets, Assets, CMDB, Knowledge, SLAs, and Support Evidence · Device Forensics
summary: A realistic tickets, assets, cmdb, knowledge, slas, and support evidence ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in auditable accessible support record system and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record requester-safe contact, user impact, scope, severity, service, asset, environment, time, symptom, baseline, reproduction, changes, evidence, owner, state, and next action.
Distinguish urgency, impact, severity, priority, response, restore, resolution, escalation, service target, business hours, pause condition, and communication commitment.
Relate physical and logical assets, configuration items, owners, locations, dependencies, versions, relationships, lifecycle, discovery, confidence, and history.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Closure Handoff
steps.instruction:
Order the Closure Handoff CompTIA A+ support HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Closure Handoff.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Closure Handoff assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Closure Handoff, keep Closure Handoff connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Duplicate tickets, mismatched assets, copied workarounds, breached targets, excess personal data, and weak closure hide the causal record. The debug variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Confirm user outcome, changed case, side effects, monitoring, prevention, asset and knowledge updates, retained uncertainty, privacy minimization, accessible communication, and acceptance.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Closure Handoff has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Closure Handoff, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Closure Handoff precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Closure Handoff, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Duplicate tickets, mismatched assets, copied workarounds, breached targets, excess personal data, and weak closure hide the causal record. The debug variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Confirm user outcome, changed case, side effects, monitoring, prevention, asset and knowledge updates, retained uncertainty, privacy minimization, accessible communication, and acceptance.

For CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Closure Handoff, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Closing a ticket means the technical symptom stopped once.

options.text: Declare CompTIA A+ support user outcome, fictional ticket, asset and environment identity, authorization, hazards, accessibility and privacy needs, baseline, and stop conditions for the debug Closure Handoff case
Predict CompTIA A+ support reproduction, timeline, competing hypotheses, bounded test outcomes, evidence quality, escalation, and rollback triggers for the debug Closure Handoff case
Run the smallest deterministic CompTIA A+ support manifest review and retain the actual observation, rejected inference, next decision, and remaining uncertainty for the debug Closure Handoff case

Change one CompTIA A+ support user, device, version, hazard, symptom, access need, or competing cause; verify repair, rollback, original and changed cases, prevention, handoff, and controlled equipment transfer limits for the debug Closure Handoff case

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Closure Handoff, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Closure Handoff precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Closure Handoff, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Duplicate tickets, mismatched assets, copied workarounds, breached targets, excess personal data, and weak closure hide the causal record. The debug variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Confirm user outcome, changed case, side effects, monitoring, prevention, asset and knowledge updates, retained uncertainty, privacy minimization, accessible communication, and acceptance.

For CompTIA A+ support Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Closure Handoff, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Closing a ticket means the technical symptom stopped once.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Closure Handoff
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Closure Handoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Duplicate tickets, mismatched assets, copied workarounds, breached targets, excess personal data, and weak closure hide the causal record. The debug variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Confirm user outcome, changed case, side effects, monitoring, prevention, asset and knowledge updates, retained uncertainty, privacy minimization, accessible communication, and acceptance.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Closure Handoff.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Closure Handoff, keep Closure Handoff connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Confirm user outcome, changed case, side effects, monitoring, prevention, asset and knowledge updates, retained uncertainty, privacy minimization, accessible communication, and acceptance.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Closure Handoff, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Closure Handoff precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Closure Handoff, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Duplicate tickets, mismatched assets, copied workarounds, breached targets, excess personal data, and weak closure hide the causal record. The debug variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Confirm user outcome, changed case, side effects, monitoring, prevention, asset and knowledge updates, retained uncertainty, privacy minimization, accessible communication, and acceptance.

For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Closure Handoff, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Closing a ticket means the technical symptom stopped once.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
